document.title: Usability Validation Report for GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
standards: IEC 60601-1-6:2010/AMD2:2020 - Medical electrical equipment - Part 1-6: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance - Collateral standard: Usability
IEC 62366-1:2015 - Application of Usability Engineering to Medical Devices
FDA Guidance - Applying Human Factors and Usability Engineering to Medical Devices
ISO 14971 - Medical Devices Application of Risk Management to Medical Devices

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability validation report for GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models GT1717C and GT1717CP.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents usability validation activities, task completion results, participant feedback, and conclusions for safety and effectiveness of use.
keyPoints: The report identifies GT1717C and GT1717CP as the model names.
The two products differ only in CsI deposition thickness and are identical in usability.
Validation activities evaluated usability in simulated real-world environments.
Fifteen radiology technicians participated in usability testing.
All nine usability tasks passed with 100% task completion.
No critical usability issues were identified in the summative evaluation.

evidence.claim: The document is a usability validation report for the GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Validation Report for GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
evidence.pageTarget: GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.pageTarget: GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: GT1717C and GT1717CP differ only in CsI deposition thickness and are identical in usability.
evidence.evidenceText: The two products differ only in CsI deposition thickness, as shown below, and are identical in usability. GT1717C : 300um GT1717CP : 500um

evidence.claim: The device is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children and is not for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: The Digital X-ray Imaging System is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children. It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic system. Not to be used for mammography.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital X-ray Imaging System Intended Use

evidence.claim: Applicable standards and guidelines include IEC 60601-1-6, IEC 62366-1, FDA human factors guidance, and ISO 14971.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-1-6 :2010/AMD2:2020 ... IEC 62366-1:2015 ... FDA Guidance - Applying Human Factors and Usability Engineering to Medical Devices ISO 14971 - Medical Devices Application of Risk Management to Medical Devices

evidence.claim: Validation activities evaluated usability of GT1717C and GT1717CP in simulated real-world environments.
evidence.evidenceText: Validation activities were conducted to evaluate the usability of the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (Ref no. GT1717C, GT1717CP) in simulated real-world environments.

evidence.claim: The user groups were radiologists and clinicians.

evidence.evidenceText: Number of Participants: 15 Demographics: Radiology technicians

evidence.claim: The summative evaluation identified no critical usability issues.
evidence.evidenceText: Outcomes: No critical usability issues were identified.

evidence.claim: Each usability task had a 100% task completion result and passed.
evidence.evidenceText: Task Completion Success Rate Criteria: A total completion rate of 100 % in each usability task. Result: 100 % (Pass)

evidence.claim: No issues were identified during participant interview sessions.
evidence.evidenceText: Based on the usability validation and evaluation report, no issues were identified during the usability testing process of the participant interview sessions.

evidence.claim: Residual usability risk was concluded to be minimized and acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability verification & evaluation results and improvement action demonstrated that residual risk regarding usability is minimized and acceptable.

reviewFlags.reason: The source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, and FDA guidance is listed, but the document itself does not identify FDA as an issuing or approving authority.
The scope states the report applies to the Portable X-ray System, while the title and validation overview identify the device as a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
Several acceptance criteria state 'over 100%' or 'more than 100%' of users, which is internally inconsistent wording for a completion criterion.
The source text contains bilingual table extraction artifacts and minor OCR/layout issues, but the key regulatory and usability facts are readable.
